title: DAC thermodynamic separation floor W_min=RT·ln(1/x) ≈19.3 kJ/mol at 420 ppm, monotone-rising as air gets more dilute
Today's DAC capture energy sits ~10× (band 3–30×) above the thermodynamic floor — finite headroom, not a ceiling
The 48 mmol/g (=J₂·φ) sorbent target is physically unreachable — 211% CO₂ by sorbent mass, ≥4.8× any measured adsorbent

title: TSA regeneration sensible heat (~100 kJ/mol, =200 at wc=0.5) dominates the 20 kJ/mol floor and scales as 1/working-capacity — the mechanism behind the 10× headroom
Compressing CO₂ to the 12 MPa pipeline costs ~11.9 kJ/mol (~60% of the capture floor); 12 MPa is correctly supercritical (> Pc 7.38 MPa)
A Sabatier-optimal CO₂ binding energy (~45–55 kJ/mol) exists — weaker gives ~0 uptake at 400 ppm, stronger gives full coverage but owes E_ads back at regeneration
1 Mt/yr DAC = 250× Climeworks (checks) but must move ~1.3e12 m³ air/yr at 420 ppm — flue needs 286× less; the binding duty is air handling, not chemistry
DAC at 9 GJ/ton is net-futile on a fossil grid — breakeven ~0.40 kgCO₂/kWh, so 0.45 grid removes ≤0 net; net removal needs clean energy or the H_010 efficiency gains
Geologic storage void is NOT the bottleneck — ~1e4 Gt is ~9× the 1095 Gt drawdown demand (100 yr at 100 Gt/yr); energy (H_014) and air handling (H_013) are the real walls
Ambient air carries ~37× more H₂O than CO₂ (50% RH/25°C), up to ~170× hot/humid — the parasitic water co-load the L0 candidate list omits, a first-order DAC energy driver

Frontier breakthrough — the closed-form harness PREDICTS measured DAC reality: bare Mg-MOF-74 (34.3 kJ/mol) predicted θ≈4e-4 at 400 ppm (fails, needs amine) yet θ≈0.09 at flue, matching measured <1 vs 3.67 mmol/g; 48 mmol/g = 24× measured best
